speaker: US Navy
quote_or_statement: Currently only 10% of shipbuilding occurs at distributed sites. The Navy aims to increase this to 50% to enhance flexibility, reduce legacy shipyard dependency, and accelerate delivery timelines.
source_url_or_doc: Navy Times reporting on US Navy FY27 Shipbuilding Plan / "Golden Fleet" announcement (May 12, 2026)
deck_relevance: POLICY-LEVEL QUOTE: The Navy itself is officially committing to a 5x increase in distributed shipbuilding (10% → 50%). Strongest possible primary-source for "outsourcing IS the strategy.".

speaker: US Navy FY27 Plan
quote_or_statement: While American shipbuilding remains the priority, the Navy will evaluate overseas options and whether allied and partner shipbuilding can supplement domestic production if U.S. industry cannot meet required timelines.

deck_relevance: Navy formally signals openness to overseas shipbuilding — the outsourcing strategy extends across borders, not just to US sub-tier vendors.

quote_or_statement: To invest more than $6.2 billion specifically to expand and stabilize the submarine industrial base through workforce development, supplier expansion, distributed shipbuilding, advanced manufacturing technologies, and productivity modernization programs.

deck_relevance: $6.2B specifically for sub IB. Confirms distributed shipbuilding is part of the official sub IB strategy.
